title: Science Mastery
description: Comprehensive science questions for all levels.

questions.text: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
What is the chemical symbol for water?
How many planets are in our solar system?
What is the closest star to Earth?
What gas do we breathe out?
What is the hardest natural substance?
What do plants use to make food?
Which organ pumps blood?
What is the freezing point of water (C)?
Which element has the atomic number 1?
What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere?
How many bones are in the adult human body?
What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
Which planet has the most moons?
What is the speed of sound?
What is the main component of the Sun?
Which blood type is the universal donor?
What is the largest organ in the human body?
Which gas is responsible for the smell of rotten eggs?
What is the center of an atom called?
What is the boiling point of water in Fahrenheit?
Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
What is the largest planet in our solar system?
Which vitamin is produced when the human body is exposed to sunlight?
What is the chemical symbol for Sodium?
What part of the plant conducts photosynthesis?
Which planet is known as the Morning Star?
What is the most common element in the universe?
How many states of matter are there (basic)?
What is the study of mushrooms called?
Which instrument measures atmospheric pressure?
What is the powerhouse of the cell?
What is the chemical symbol for Silver?
Which bird is the only one that can fly backwards?
What is the chemical symbol for Iron?
Which planet is closest to the Sun?
What is the unit of electrical resistance?
What is the largest species of shark?
What is the chemical symbol for Potassium?
Which particle is proposed by the Standard Model to explain why some elementary particles have mass?
What is the primary product of the CNO cycle in stellar nucleosynthesis?
Which law describes the relationship between the heat capacity of a solid and its vibrational modes at low temperatures?
What organic functional group is characterized by a carbon atom double-bonded to an oxygen atom and single-bonded to a hydroxyl group?
In molecular biology, which enzyme is responsible for synthesizing short RNA sequences called primers during DNA replication?
What is the main astronomical reference point for measuring right ascension?
Which thermodynamic state function represents the maximum reversible work that can be performed by a system at constant temperature and pressure?
What type of gene mutation results in a premature stop codon in the transcribed mRNA?
Which effect describes the shift in the spectral lines of an atom when subjected to an external electric field?
What is the primary geological mineral that makes up the bulk of the Earth's lower mantle?
Which class of organic compounds undergoes the Diels-Alder reaction?
What is the term for the minimum energy required to remove an electron from the surface of a metal in the photoelectric effect?
Which neurological pathway is primarily degenerated in patients with Parkinson's disease?
What quantum number determines the orientation of an orbital in space?
Which cosmological epoch refers to the period when the universe cooled enough for electrons to bind to protons, forming neutral hydrogen?
What is the correct IUPAC name for the simplest chiral alkane?
In immunology, which class of antibody is secreted in pentameric form and is the first to respond during an primary infection?
Which physical principle explains why a spinning top precesses instead of falling over?
Which complex in the mitochondrial electron transport chain is also part of the citric acid cycle (Krebs cycle)?
What is the primary coordinate system used by astronomers to specify the positions of stars relative to the Earth's equator?
Which mechanism explains the superconductivity of materials at extremely low temperatures via electron pairing?
What type of intermolecular force is primarily responsible for the unique high boiling point of water?
In biochemistry, what is the term for an inactive enzyme precursor that requires proteolytic cleavage to become active?
Which constant represents the quantization of electromagnetic radiation, relating a photon's energy to its frequency?
What is the name of the hypothetical boundary surrounding a black hole beyond which nothing can escape?
Which organic reaction converts a primary amide into a primary amine with one fewer carbon atom?
What is the major structural protein found in animal connective tissues, characterized by a triple-helix structure?
Which mechanical quantity is defined as the rotational analog of mass, measuring an object's resistance to angular acceleration?
Which region of the human brain is the primary site for the consolidation of short-term memory into long-term memory?
What is the term for the phase transition where a substance passes directly from the solid to the gaseous state without passing through the liquid state?
